title: MCP-A follow_up Response
description: Response for the follow_up primitive (SPEC §4). Same shape as the query response, plus a `status` field for polling long-running compilations. answer_id may be the same (refinement) or new (polling completed); routing_decision SHOULD indicate re-route vs. reuse.

answer.description: Compiled natural-language answer (same semantics as query.response).

structured.description: Typed structured payload, present only when a response_schema applied to the underlying answer.

structured_schema_ref.description: Which schema/ontology the structured payload conforms to.

answer_id.description: May equal the prior answer_id (refinement) or be a new ID (polling completed). MUST be present.

recommended_tool.description: Optional drill path to a specific domain/tool.

reason.description: Why this tool might provide more detail.

routing_decision.description: Routing detail; SHOULD indicate whether this was a re-route or reuse of prior routing (see reused_prior_routing).

status.description: Compilation status for polling (SPEC §4). MUST be present for long-running compiles.

timestamp.description: RFC3339 timestamp when the answer was compiled.

is_draft.description: True when the answer is still an incomplete draft (e.g., poll still pending).
